cataloging_subject.label: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged intellectual unit is the bounded medieval compilation transmitted in Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. B. 408 as represented by Andrew Clark's 1905-1911 edition: a fragmentary commination text, a group of devotional verses, a metrical Sarum kalendar, and the main Middle English cartulary prepared as an English key to Godstow Abbey's Latin register. The main cartulary is itself a documentary compilation, not one charter and not 903 independent works. Clark's 1-903 numbers are editorial cross-reference positions: the XML contains 897 numbered divisions, with positions 541-545, 613-614, and 828 absent and 143 and 827 duplicated. The physical codices, Latin source cartulary, Clark edition and apparatus, two U-M print-source scan carriers, U-M encoding, complete Internet Archive edition scan, and repository XML remain separate lineage layers.

catalog_summary.title: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English translator-abridger or translators and anonymous liturgical and devotional authors or versifiers; prepared for the Benedictine nuns of Godstow Abbey
catalog_summary.editor: Andrew Clark, with collation assistance from Herbert Hurst
catalog_summary.date: Surviving compilation copied after 1467–c. 1480; individual component composition and translation dates remain partly unresolved; Clark edition published 1905-1911
catalog_summary.region: Godstow and Oxford, England; documentary estates arranged across fifteen county or city regions

titles.value: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery
notes: The preferred title is Clark's editorial title for the compilation, not a claim that the prefixed pieces are part of the English cartulary proper.

titles.value: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, Written about 1450
notes: This reproduces the edition title. Its 'about 1450' date is retained as title text, not adopted over the current Bodleian dating of the surviving codex.

scope.description: The main Middle English cartulary only

notes: The phrase occurs in the prologue heading and distinguishes the cartulary from the prefixed commination, devotional, and kalendar components.

agents.name: Anonymous Godstow English-register translator-abridger or translators
notes: The prologue presents a singular 'poor brother and well-wisher,' while Clark's analysis treats the register as translated and abridged with varying practice. The personal identity and number of contributors remain unresolved.

agents.name: Anonymous authors and versifiers of the prefixed pieces
notes: A cataloging collective for creators of the commination, devotional verse, and metrical kalendar; it does not assert single authorship across those components.

agents.name: Andrew Clark
notes: 1856-1922. Editor, transcriber, arranger, commentator, and collator of the EETS edition.

agents.name: Herbert Hurst
notes: Clark credits Hurst with repeated Public Record Office comparison of doubtful places and deeds, an annotated witness list for Oxford deeds, and local Godstow knowledge.

agents.name: John Lydgate
notes: Macray's 1862 catalog places the kalendar attribution in brackets; Clark and the current abbreviated Bodleian record do not adopt it.

scope.description: Middle English translation and abridgement of the Latin Godstow register

notes: This responsibility does not extend to the prefixed liturgical and devotional pieces.

scope.description: Compilation of an English documentary key for the nuns of Godstow from the Latin register

notes: The compiler followed the Latin register's broad order but translated by sense, abridged, and sometimes omitted or misread source material.
No shared personal author is asserted across the three components.

assertion.notes: The attribution is not corroborated by Clark's edition or the current abbreviated Bodleian description.

form_statements.description: The prefixed devotional and kalendar components are verse, while the commination and main cartulary are principally prose.
The commination fragment and documentary cartulary are prose, notwithstanding embedded formulae and editorial notes.
The devotional group has 36 seven-line stanzas and the kalendar has 365 daily lines in 52 stanzas, the last stanza having eight lines.

subject_statements.label: Monastic estates, landholding, and property rights
Charters, deeds, grants, confirmations, and privileges
Benedictine nuns and female monastic administration

summaries.value: A heterogeneous Middle English compilation made for or preserved by the Benedictine nuns of Godstow. Its prefixed material comprises a fragmentary commination text with canon-law citations, devotional verse including an Invocation to the Cross and metrical exercises, and a 365-line Sarum kalendar. The principal component is an English translation and abridged documentary key to Godstow's 1404 Latin cartulary, preserving grants, confirmations, privileges, estate records, and other instruments concerning the abbey's property and administration.

Clark's printed target runs from pages 1-683. The prefixed commination occupies pp. 1-3, devotional verses pp. 4-12, and kalendar pp. 13-24. The English cartulary begins with six prologue and foundation units on pp. 25-32; estate deeds follow on pp. 33-639 in fifteen region divisions; seven deeds for places unidentified when first sent to press occupy pp. 640-643; and thirty-eight general charters of bishops, sovereigns, and popes occupy pp. 644-683.

The current Bodleian description compresses the codex into two msItems, the cartulary and devotional verses; Clark's edition and the XML expose the commination, devotional, kalendar, and cartulary boundaries more fully. Godstow Abbey is the associated institution and intended beneficiary, not a blanket personal author. Clark's editorial rearrangement, Latin collation, supplied English summaries, arguments, notes, and modern dates belong to the edition layer. The main English cartulary is a translation or version of TNA E 164/20; that relation does not extend to the three prefixed components.

extent.description: Five intellectual items are cataloged within four top-level medieval components: one prose commination fragment, two devotional verse pieces, one metrical kalendar, and one prose documentary compilation. The XML's 897 numbered documentary divisions and the kalendar's 365 ROW elements are structural and editorial metrics, not independent intellectual items. The XML has 5 DIV1, 7 DIV2, 42 DIV3, 170 DIV4, 572 DIV5, 355 DIV6, and 714 PB elements. Its documentary divisions comprise six introductory units and 891 deed divisions and occupy a 1-903 editorial sequence with eight absent numbers (541-545, 613-614, 828) and two duplicated numbers (143, 827), yielding 895 unique values. Devotional verse contributes 36 seven-line stanzas and 252 lines; the metrical kalendar contributes 52 stanzas and 365 daily lines, for 88 verse groups and 617 verse lines in total.

content_parts.label: Articles of Excommunication and form of excommunication
content_parts.summary: The surviving latter portion of a commination text: a catalogue of excommunicable offences supported by abbreviated Latin canon-law citations, followed by a form of excommunication. The opening leaves are missing.

locators.value: ff. 1-2 in Macray and Clark; opening material defective

notes: Clark says two leaves had been cut out; Macray says only a small corner of the first leaf remains. The precise reconciliation is an open physical-witness question.

content_parts.label: Devotional pieces
content_parts.summary: A zero-count grouping section containing two verse items: a twenty-eight-line acrostic Invocation to the Cross and metrical devotional exercises on the sign of the cross, principal prayers, confession, night prayer, thanksgiving, and prayer for the dead.

content_parts.label: Invocation to the Cross
content_parts.summary: A twenty-eight-line acrostic devotional poem in four seven-line stanzas, introduced as the ABC of devotion.

content_parts.label: Metrical versions of general acts of devotion
content_parts.summary: Thirty-two seven-line stanzas expanding the sign of the cross, Pater noster, Ave Maria, Creed, confession and absolution prayers, commendation of the soul, blessings and thanks, recompense, and prayer for departed souls.

content_parts.label: Metrical Sarum kalendar
content_parts.summary: A four-column kalendar giving Golden Numbers, dominical letters, Roman dates, and a metrical Sarum church calendar. One line is assigned to each day; the final stanza expands to eight lines for the 365th day.

content_parts.label: The English Register of Godstow
content_parts.summary: The main Middle English documentary cartulary, presented as an English key to the Latin Godstow register for the nuns' estate administration. It combines a prologue and foundation sequence, estate records, supplementary place records, and general charters.

locators.value: begins fol. I(13) and ends with leaf 198 blank in Clark's transcription

notes: Its 897 encoded numbered units are representation divisions within one documentary compilation.

content_parts.label: Prologue, foundation, and early foundation records
content_parts.summary: Six numbered units comprising the English prologue, a chronicle of the foundation, a charter, and related foundation or institutional records.

content_parts.label: Godstow estates
content_parts.summary: Eight hundred forty-six deed divisions arranged by Clark under ninety-three estate heads in fifteen region divisions: Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Dorsetshire, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Northamptonshire, Oxfordshire, Oxford city, Somerset, Sussex, Warwickshire, Wiltshire, and Yorkshire.

locators.value: 33-639; editorial numbers 7-858 subject to known gaps and duplicates

notes: The county-parish and chronological order is Clark's editorial rearrangement, not the order of Rawl. B. 408 or E 164/20.

content_parts.label: Places unidentified when the manuscript was first sent to press
content_parts.summary: Seven supplementary deed divisions for places that were unidentified when the manuscript was first sent to press, some subsequently identified in the edition.

content_parts.label: General charters
content_parts.summary: Thirty-eight general charters and privileges associated with bishops of Lincoln, English sovereigns, and popes.

related_works.title: The Latin Cartulary of Godstow Abbey
notes: The relation applies only to part:english-register. The English version translates and abridges the Latin cartulary; the three prefixed components are not asserted to derive from it.

The current Discovery record dates the archival item 1404-15th century and notes later insertions and loose rubricated documents. Clark attributes the register's 1404 compilation at least partly to Alice of Henley, while modern scholarship reviewed by Maddicott argues that Alice of Eaton probably wrote it in 1403-1404.

evidence.citation: Complete element, numbering, page, verse, and hierarchy audit of pinned aha2738 XML against Clark's edition scan.
evidence.locator: All DIV1-DIV6, PB, LG, L, ROW, HEAD, ARGUMENT, MILESTONE, and N attributes
evidence.summary: Counts 5 DIV1, 7 DIV2, 42 DIV3, 170 DIV4, 572 DIV5, 355 DIV6, 714 PB, 36 LG, 252 L, 365 kalendar rows, 15 region divisions, 100 estate heads, and 891 deed divisions. It establishes 897 numbered units in a 1-903 sequence with missing 541-545, 613-614, and 828 and duplicated 143 and 827, and maps the four top-level components and main-cartulary subdivisions to printed pagination.

evidence.citation: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, ed. Andrew Clark, EETS Original Series 129-130 and 142 (London, 1905-1911), complete final-bound Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 7, 9, 15-26, 151-833, and 834-872; printed pp. i-cxliii and 1-722, especially forewords ix-xx and target text pp. 1-683
evidence.summary: Verifies the final 1911 title and binding state, three-part publication, component descriptions, editor's kalendar conjecture, Rawl. B. 408 and E 164/20 source statements, all-document transcription claim, twelve missing Rawl leaves, Latin collation and supplied summaries, editorial rearrangement, Hurst's assistance, complete printed target, additional notes, and indexes.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Rawl. B. 408, manuscript_8035, pinned TEI revision 30eeba18af5a67e9585a2477b48a0847607228a3.
evidence.locator: msIdentifier; msContents items 1-2; physDesc; origin; provenance; recordHist; revisionDesc
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark and ARKs, cartulary and devotional-verse msItems, Middle English, parchment support, Oxford origin with structured bounds notBefore 1467 and notAfter 1480 and display 'After 1467 – c. 1480', Godstow provenance, Ware-Clarendon-Rawlinson ownership, 1755 Bodleian acquisition, and the record's abbreviation from Macray.

evidence.citation: William D. Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecae Bodleianae, Part V, fascicle 1 (Oxford, 1862), Rawl. B. 408 at cols. 665-666, complete ORA scan.
evidence.summary: Supports the 224-folio parchment codex, Ware and Clarendon provenance, anonymous English translator and Alice Henley dedication, three prefixed content descriptions, two brief incipits, bracketed Lydgate attribution, Wood sketch, and two specifically noted missing-leaf positions. It is supporting catalog description rather than a target representation.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AHA2738.0001.001, The English Register of Godstow Nunnery.
evidence.locator: About this Item; Rights/Permissions; Pages; printed page 25 and editorial note on numbers 1-903
evidence.summary: Confirms the current structured-text item, Clark edition citation, public full-text access, per-page View Page links, editorial 1-903 numbering statement, and current U-M public-domain and No Copyright-United States labels.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Discovery record C2960643, E 164/20, complete API record checked 16 July 2026.
evidence.locator: citableReference; coveringDates; scopeContent; closureStatus; heldBy; digitised; note
evidence.summary: Confirms E 164/20, covering dates 1404-15th century, open status, custody at The National Archives in Kew, a Godstow Abbey cartulary with later insertions and loose rubricated documents, no digitised copy, and notes citing Clark and binding reference E 166/1/2.

evidence.citation: University of Oxford Community History, 'Godstow Cartularies - E 164/20, 1404, TNA, London and Rawlinson MS B 408,' 1 December 2025.
evidence.locator: section 2, lines identifying both Godstow cartularies
evidence.summary: Provides current institutional corroboration of the two-cartulary distinction, exact TNA shelfmark E 164/20 and 1404 date, Rawlinson MS B 408, and the differing English and Latin documentary representations.

evidence.citation: J. R. Maddicott, review of The Latin Cartulary of Godstow Abbey, ed. Emilie Amt, English Historical Review 130.547 (2015), 1532-1533, doi:10.1093/ehr/cev262.
evidence.summary: Confirms that the English and Latin books are two versions of the Godstow cartulary, that almost all Latin charters are known from the English cartulary but the Latin preserves original language and witness lists, and reports Amt's argument that Alice of Eaton probably wrote the Latin cartulary in 1403-1404.

open_questions.question: When was the English cartulary translated and compiled, and does the prologue's singular 'poor brother' represent one translator or a more complex collaborative process?
next_steps: Consult current linguistic and codicological scholarship on Rawl. B. 408 beyond Clark's 1911 analysis.
Keep the current witness date after 1467–c. 1480 separate from a translation date until direct evidence supports the latter.
Test Clark's vocabulary and error patterns at passage level before deciding whether to model multiple translators.

open_questions.question: What evidence, if any, supports Macray's bracketed attribution of the metrical kalendar to John Lydgate?
next_steps: Trace the attribution through Macray's sources and modern Lydgate scholarship.
Compare the Rawl. B. 408 kalendar with Douce MS 322 and cataloged Lydgate kalendars.
Retain anonymous authorship as preferred unless corroborating evidence is found.

open_questions.question: How should Macray's two specifically located missing leaves be reconciled with Clark's statement that twelve closely written leaves had been cut from Rawl. B. 408?
next_steps: Inspect the codex or a complete folio-by-folio surrogate, including old Roman and modern Arabic foliations.
Map every Clark Rawl-MS and Exchequer-MS folio milestone around supplied passages.
Do not infer that either count alone describes every physical loss without codicological reconciliation.

open_questions.question: Can all 897 encoded numbered divisions be crosswalked item by item to Rawl. B. 408, E 164/20, and Clark's 1-903 editorial positions?
next_steps: Build a passage-level table from XML NODE, editorial N, printed page, Rawl folio milestones, and Exchequer folio milestones.
Resolve the duplicated 143 and 827 and absent 541-545, 613-614, and 828 values against the printed edition before item-level cataloging.
Keep current content parts aggregated until that audit is complete.

notes: Godstow Abbey is modeled as the intended institutional beneficiary and subject, not as a single personal author of every component or deed.
The main English cartulary is a translation and abridged key to TNA E 164/20; the relation does not apply to the commination, devotional verse, or metrical kalendar.

Clark says all documents surviving in the English manuscript are printed, but also supplies English summaries from the Latin register where Rawl leaves are missing and uses the Latin to correct omissions and errors. Those composite edition practices belong to lineage.
The 897 XML divisions summarize a 1-903 editorial reference system with known gaps and duplicates; they are not treated as 897 independently authored works.
The four-component hierarchy is complete at the chosen aggregation level, while individual deed-level metadata and full witness crosswalk remain future work.
